source.definition: to move or proceed on foot or by any means from one place to another

explanation.sourceText: The English verb "go" originates from Old English "gān" and is related to other Germanic languages' words meaning to move or walk.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „go” provine din engleza veche „gān” și este înrudit cu alte cuvinte din limbile germanice care înseamnă a se mișca sau a merge.

source.additional: Irregular verb. Commonly used to describe movement and travel.

explanation.sourceText: The origin of the Romanian verb "a merge" is debated; it has traditionally been linked to the Latin "mergere" (to immerse, to sink), but this derivation is uncertain.
explanation.targetText: Originea verbului românesc „a merge” este controversată; deși a fost asociat tradițional cu latinescul „mergere” („a scufunda”), această etimologie este nesigură și disputată.

explanation.sourceText: Can also mean to travel a certain distance.
explanation.targetText: Poate însemna și a parcurge o distanță.

explanation.sourceText: Means to move on foot or to walk.
explanation.targetText: Înseamnă a se deplasa pe jos sau a merge.

explanation.sourceText: Used in various idiomatic expressions, e.g., "I am doing well."
explanation.targetText: Este folosit în diverse expresii, de exemplu „Mie îmi merge bine“.

examples.sourceText: He often goes to the cinema.
We go for a walk together.
They go to the park every Sunday.
examples.targetText: Ei merg în parc în fiecare duminică.

examples.sourceText: He went to work on foot every day.
examples.targetText: El mergea în fiecare zi pe jos la muncă.

examples.sourceText: I would go if I had time.
examples.targetText: Aș merge dacă aș avea timp.

examples.sourceText: The person who is going is my friend.
examples.targetText: Persoana care merge este prietenul meu.

learningTips.text: Remember "go" as the basic verb for movement—think of the phrase "Ready, set, go!" to connect it with action.
The Romanian "a merge" sounds like "a merge" in English, which means to proceed or move forward, helping you recall its meaning.
Use the command form "Mergi!" to remember it means "Go!" in Romanian, similar to the English imperative.
Think of "merge" in English computing terms (to combine or move together) to link with the idea of moving or going somewhere.
